what: End-to-End Pipeline for Shift-Share (Bartik) Instrumental Variables
title: End-to-End Pipeline for Shift-Share (Bartik) Instrumental Variables
text: Shift-share / Bartik IV analysis R currently spread across several single-purpose tools, data conventions. ssBartik connects steps one consistent workflow: () construct variables → (b) diagnose → (c) estimate → (d) infer → (e) visualize, organized around two identification routes modern literature (.e., exogenous shift exogenous share approaches). pick identification route single argument (exogenous = "share" "shift" – required, since fixes inference, controls diagnostics), everything downstream follows simple functions.

title: End-to-End Pipeline for Shift-Share (Bartik) Instrumental Variables
text: ssBartik turns estimation diagnostic results publication-ready figures tables. examples demonstrate package’s main outputs using sample datasets. first model figure follows visualization Goldsmith-Pinkham, Sorkin, Swift (2020).

title: End-to-End Pipeline for Shift-Share (Bartik) Instrumental Variables
text: design hand, credibility check finished single call.       returns small object readable print() method; checks run automatically inside ssbartik() / ssb_pipeline() pass corresponding arguments (pre_y, placebo_y, shock_covariates, covariates).
code: d   <- ssb_design(df$data, df$shares, df$shocks,                   controls = "w1", weights = "pop", exogenous = "share") rot <- ssb_rotemberg(d)                    # Rotemberg-weight decomposition plot(rot, n = 6)                           # see below est <- ssb_estimate(d)                     # route-appropriate SEs (share: EHW/cluster; shift: AKM/AKM0) ssb_plot_ci(est) # instrument strength & internal consistency ssb_first_stage(d)               # standard robust F + exposure-robust "effective" F ssb_equivalence(d)               # location-level SSIV == shock-level IV (sanity check) ssb_overid(d)                    # joint validity of the share instruments (Sargan–Hansen J; share route) # identifying-assumption checks ssb_shock_balance(d, shock_covariates = sc)  # shocks vs. pre-determined characteristics ssb_pretrend(d, pre_y = "y_pre")             # does exposure predict pre-trends? ssb_placebo(d, placebo_y = "y_plac")         # full IV on an outcome that shouldn't move ssb_ri(d, R = 999, block = "grp")            # AR-style randomization inference (shocks permuted within blocks) # sensitivity to influential shocks ws<- ssb_weight_summary(d, covariates = "w1")     # who carries the weight? (α vs. βₖ, F, covariates) plot(ws) ssb_drop_top(d, n = 5)                        # re-estimate without the top-weight shocks ssb_recenter(d, method = "permute", block = "grp")     # block-wise recentering (within-block mean shock)

what: ssBartik: End-to-End Pipeline for Shift-Share (Bartik) IV Designs — ssBartik-package
title: ssBartik: End-to-End Pipeline for Shift-Share (Bartik) IV Designs — ssBartik-package
text: Shift-share / Bartik instrumental-variable analysis R spread across several single-purpose packages: ShiftShareSE (Kolesar) AKM inference, bartik.weight (jjchern) Rotemberg weights, ssaggregate (Butts) shock-level aggregation. ssBartik brings construction, diagnostics, estimation, exposure-robust inference publication-ready visualisation one consistent workflow, organised around two identification routes modern literature (exogenous shares vs. exogenous shifts).

title: ssBartik: End-to-End Pipeline for Shift-Share (Bartik) IV Designs — ssBartik-package
text: single entry point [ssb_design()] (one-call [ssbartik()]). Choose route `exogenous`; instrument constructed identically either way, flag governs diagnostics controls apply.

title: ssBartik: End-to-End Pipeline for Shift-Share (Bartik) IV Designs — ssBartik-package
text: Built gratitude work Michal Kolesar (ShiftShareSE), Kyle Butts (`ssaggregate`), Junjie (JJ) Chern (`bartik.weight`) Paul Goldsmith-Pinkham (`bartik-weight`, original Stata implementation). Core methods: Adao, Kolesar & Morales (2019, QJE); Borusyak, Hull & Jaravel (2022, REStud), Borusyak, Hull & Jaravel (2025, "Practical Guide Shift-Share Instruments") Borusyak, Hull & Jaravel (2025, "Design-based identification formula instruments: review", Econometrics Journal 28(1), 83-108); Goldsmith-Pinkham, Sorkin & Swift (2020, AER).

what: Aggregate a shift-share design to the shock (shifter) level — ssb_aggregate
title: Aggregate a shift-share design to the shock (shifter) level — ssb_aggregate
text: Collapses unit-level design one row per shock, following Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel (2022) equivalence. controls partialled outcome treatment (weighted FWL), shock \(n\) gets exposure weight \(s_n=\sum_i e_i s_{}\) exposure-weighted means \(\bar y_n=\sum_i e_i s_{}\tilde y_i/s_n\) \(\bar x_n\) similarly. Running IV \(\bar y_n\) \(\bar x_n\) instrument \(g_n\) weights \(s_n\) reproduces location-level shift-share estimate exactly (see [ssb_equivalence()]).

code: ssb_design(   data,   shares,   shocks,   y = "y",   x = "x",   location = "location",   sector = "sector",   time = NULL,   controls = NULL,   weights = NULL,   cluster = NULL,   share_col = "share",   shock_col = "shock",   exogenous )

title: Define a shift-share (Bartik) IV design — ssb_design
text: data unit-level `data.frame`: one row per location (location-period). Must contain `y`, `x`, `location`, `controls`, `weights`, `cluster` columns referenced . shares long `data.frame` exposure shares columns `location`, `sector`, share column (`share_col`), `time` panels. shocks `data.frame` shocks columns `sector`, shock column (`shock_col`), `time` panels. y, x Column names (strings) outcome endogenous treatment. location, sector Column names unit sector identifiers. time Optional column name period identifier (present `data`, `shares` `shocks`) panel designs. controls Optional character vector control columns `data`. Numeric columns enter linearly; factor character columns expanded dummies, period region fixed effects can supplied factors. shift route, period fixed effects (interacted sum exposure shares) added automatically panels — shocks compared within periods; share route panels, supply period fixed effects . weights Optional column name regression weights `data`. cluster Optional column name clustering variable `data`. share_col Name exposure-share column `shares` (default `"share"`). shock_col Name shock (shift) column `shocks` (default `"shock"`). exogenous **Required.** identification route design rests : `"shift"` (exogenous shocks) `"share"` (exogenous shares). `"shock"`/`"shares"` accepted aliases. default: route determines automatic controls, appropriate standard errors relevant diagnostics, must chosen deliberately.

title: Define a shift-share (Bartik) IV design — ssb_design
text: object class `ssb_design`.

title: Define a shift-share (Bartik) IV design — ssb_design
text: **instrument constructed identically** whichever identification route take; `exogenous` argument governs *inference*, *diagnostics* *controls* appropriate downstream (see [ssb_estimate()], [ssb_pipeline()]). Set `exogenous = "share"` exogenous-shares route (Goldsmith-Pinkham, Sorkin Swift 2020; Rotemberg-weight diagnostics conventional inference) `exogenous = "shift"` exogenous-shocks route (Borusyak, Hull Jaravel 2022; Adao, Kolesar Morales 2019; shock-level diagnostics exposure-robust inference). route identification assumption, **must specified explicitly** – default. shift route package adds Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel controls automatically: incomplete shares per-unit **sum exposure shares**, panels sum **interacted period fixed effects** (controlling overall sum enough panel – shocks must compared within periods; complete shares interaction reduces period fixed effects). automatic columns pruned anything already spanned `controls`, supplying period fixed effects harmless.

title: Define a shift-share (Bartik) IV design — ssb_design

title: Overidentification test across the share instruments (Sargan-Hansen) — ssb_overid
text: J statistic comes efficient two-step GMM heteroskedasticity-robust weight matrix (cluster-robust design `cluster` variable), accounts fact just-identified estimates estimated ** sample mutually correlated. Earlier versions reported precision-weighted Cochran Q, valid estimates independent; replaced. Following Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel JEP practical guide, several estimators common coefficient available: `"2sls"` efficient two-step `"gmm"` natural number instruments \(K\) modest, `"liml"` `"jive"` (JIVE1) guard many-instrument bias \(K\) large relative sample. `estimator = "auto"` (default) picks 2SLS \(K \le \max(3, 0.05\,n)\) LIML otherwise, message. shares sum one (sum shares controlled) residualised share instruments exactly collinear; redundant columns dropped automatically, complete-shares design \(K\) sectors uses \(K-1\) instruments test \(K-2\) degrees freedom, matching usual leave-one-share-formulation.

title: Overidentification test across the share instruments (Sargan-Hansen) — ssb_overid
code: sim <- ssb_simulate(n_loc = 80, n_sec = 10, seed = 1) d <- ssb_design(sim$data, sim$shares, sim$shocks, exogenous = "share") ssb_overid(d, estimator = "2sls") #> <ssBartik overidentification test (Sargan-Hansen)> #>   estimator : 2SLS over 9 share instruments (1 collinear dropped) #>   beta = 1.5851   se = 0.1487   [1.294, 1.877] #>   Hansen J = 5.77 on 8 df,  p = 0.6731 #>   instruments dropped: 0 (min_F / degenerate); weak (F<10): 9 #>   small p => reject joint validity of the share instruments #>   (exclusion failure for some shares OR treatment-effect heterogeneity)

Pre-trend ([ssb_pretrend()]) placebo ([ssb_placebo()]) checks run routes `pre_y` / `placebo_y` supplied, route-appropriate headline inference. Arguments belonging route (`shock_covariates` share route, `covariates` shift route) skipped message rather silently mixed .

title: Recenter the shocks (Borusyak & Hull) — ssb_recenter
text: Recentering removes expected instrument implied shock-assignment process, identification comes deviations shocks (conditional) mean. Two methods: `"demean"` (default): subtract single exposure-weighted mean shock         \(\bar g\). Leaves point estimate unchanged makes         identifying variation explicit. `"permute"`: subtract *block-specific simple average* shock, .e.         recenter within exchangeability groups. uniform within-block         permutation every cell block equally likely receive         block's shocks, \(E[g_n]\) unweighted within-block         mean; subtracting gives expectation instrument         assignment process (Borusyak & Hull), computed analytically.         `block` recenters grand unweighted mean. randomization-inference p-values based permutation idea, see [ssb_ri()].

title: Randomization-inference (placebo-shock) test — ssb_ri
text: statistic Anderson-Rubin-style: reduced-form coefficient \(y - \beta_0 x\) reconstructed instrument, \(\beta_0 =\) `null`. constant-effects null \(\beta = \beta_0\) (plus exclusion restriction), \(y - \beta_0 x\) respond shocks assigned, permutation distribution statistic *exact* given exchangeability encoded `block`. Permuting IV ratio (holding observed treatment fixed) ** exact — treatment also responds shocks first stage, placebo draws weak first stages give ratio heavy tails — function . **exchangeability assumption stronger shocks -good--random.** Permutation validity requires shocks (within `block`) *exchangeable* — particular, identically distributed reordering. Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel framework assumes shocks -good--randomly assigned (mean-independent unobservables), allows variances higher moments differ across cells; weaker assumption randomization inference can - -reject. Use `block` group shocks plausibly comparable draws (keep permutations within periods panels), treat RI p-value complement — substitute — exposure-robust inference [ssb_estimate()] / [ssb_shock_iv()].

title: Randomization-inference (placebo-shock) test — ssb_ri

what: Rotemberg weights for a Bartik instrument — ssb_rotemberg
title: Rotemberg weights for a Bartik instrument — ssb_rotemberg
text: Decomposes shift-share 2SLS estimate weighted sum just-identified estimates use sector's share single instrument, following Goldsmith-Pinkham, Sorkin Swift (2020): $$\hat\beta = \sum_n \hat\alpha_n \hat\beta_n,\qquad       \hat\alpha_n = \frac{g_n\, \tilde s_n' \tilde x}{\sum_{n'} g_{n'}\,       \tilde s_{n'}' \tilde x},$$ tildes denote residualisation controls (, panels, sector-cells sector \(\times\) period pairs). weights \(\hat\alpha_n\) sum one measure sensitivity \(\hat\beta\) misspecification sector's instrument; small number large weights warning sign. Unlike Goodman-Bacon weights, negative Rotemberg weights automatically problematic.

title: One-call shift-share analysis — ssbartik
code: # Bring your own data; this is a small synthetic design for illustration. set.seed(1) n_loc <- 60L; n_sec <- 8L shares <- expand.grid(location = seq_len(n_loc), sector = seq_len(n_sec)) shares$share <- stats::runif(nrow(shares)) tot <- tapply(shares$share, shares$location, sum) shares$share <- shares$share / tot[as.character(shares$location)] shocks <- data.frame(sector = seq_len(n_sec), shock = stats::rnorm(n_sec)) Z <- tapply(shares$share, list(shares$location, shares$sector), sum) Z[is.na(Z)] <- 0 inst <- as.numeric(Z %*% shocks$shock) dat <- data.frame(location = seq_len(n_loc),                   x = 4 * inst + stats::rnorm(n_loc, sd = 0.3)) dat$y <- 1.2 * dat$x + stats::rnorm(n_loc, sd = 0.3) res <- ssbartik(dat, shares, shocks, exogenous = "share") #> ssb_overid(): estimator = 'auto' resolved to 'liml' (K = 7 instruments, n = 60; see ?ssb_overid). res #> == ssBartik result ============================================== #> route : exogenous SHARE #>  #> <ssBartik estimate> #>   route         : exogenous SHARE -> conventional (EHW / cluster) inference #>   first-stage F : 324.9 #>  method estimate std.error conf.low conf.high note #>     EHW     1.23    0.0382     1.15       1.3      #>  #> <ssBartik first-stage strength> #>   standard robust F         : 324.9 #>   effective (exposure) F    : 1308.9 #>  #> <ssBartik overidentification test (Sargan-Hansen)> #>   estimator : LIML over 7 share instruments (1 collinear dropped) #>   beta = 1.2218   se = 0.0396   [1.144, 1.300] #>   Hansen J = 5.15 on 6 df,  p = 0.5242 #>   instruments dropped: 0 (min_F / degenerate); weak (F<10): 4 #>   small p => reject joint validity of the share instruments #>   (exclusion failure for some shares OR treatment-effect heterogeneity) #>  #> <ssBartik Rotemberg weights> #>   overall beta_hat : 1.2263 #>   sum positive alpha : 1.000   sum negative alpha : 0.000 #>   largest weight   : alpha = 0.439 (6) #>   ! one share instrument carries |alpha| = 0.44; check robustness via ssb_drop_top() #>   shocks demeaned (overall, exposure-weighted) before weighting -- GPSS/BHJ normalisation #>   top 5 sectors by |alpha|: #>  sector  alpha beta     F #>       6 0.4390 1.21 36.89 #>       5 0.1488 1.32 12.01 #>       2 0.1346 1.11 10.41 #>       1 0.1144 1.34 11.99 #>       4 0.0788 1.27  6.02 #>   (negative weights are not by themselves a red flag; see GPSS 2020) #>  #> <ssBartik Rotemberg-weight summary> #>   largest weight: alpha = 0.439 (6) #>   cor(alpha, beta_k) = -0.22   cor(alpha, F) = 0.99 #>   top share instruments by |alpha|: #>  sector  alpha beta     F      g #>       6 0.4390 1.21 36.89 -1.901 #>       5 0.1488 1.32 12.01  1.263 #>       2 0.1346 1.11 10.41  1.134 #>       1 0.1144 1.34 11.99  0.807 #>       4 0.0788 1.27  6.02 -0.779 #>  #> [leave-one-out] overall beta = 1.2263  #>  sector  alpha beta_drop #>       6 0.4390      1.24 #>       5 0.1488      1.21 #>       2 0.1346      1.24 #>       1 0.1144      1.21 #>       4 0.0788      1.22 #> ================================================================= # \donttest{ autoplot(res)                       # headline Rotemberg figure  autoplot(res$estimate)              # CI comparison  # }

title: ssBartik 0.2.0
text: ssb_design() / ssbartik(): exogenous now required (default). identification route fixes automatic controls, standard errors diagnostics, must chosen deliberately. ssb_estimate() (ssb_placebo(), ssb_drop_top()): default methods now follow route – conventional ehw (+ cluster cluster variable set) share route, exposure-robust akm / akm0 shift route. Explicit methods = c(...) still overrides, printed table names route. (Review point 1.) ssb_overid() now reports Sargan–Hansen J efficient two-step GMM robust (cluster-robust) weight matrix, replacing precision-weighted Cochran Q, treated mutually correlated just-identified estimates independent. Estimators common coefficient: "2sls", efficient "gmm", many-instrument-robust "liml" / "jive" ("auto" picks 2SLS LIML K/n). Collinear residualised shares pruned automatically (complete designs use K−1 instruments, df = K−2). Output fields changed accordingly; ssb_plot_overid(), format() plot() methods updated. (Point 3.) ssb_pipeline() reports route-appropriate statistics: Rotemberg weights / weight summary / overidentification / share balance share route; equivalence / shock summary / shock balance shift route. Arguments belonging route skipped message. (Point 2.) ssb_share_balance() now tests sectors largest |Rotemberg weight| (shares drive estimate), largest average exposure, returns alpha. (Point 4.)

title: ssBartik 0.2.0
text: Exposure-robust (shock-level) variances ssb_shock_iv(), ssb_first_stage() ssb_pretrend() now use shocks residualised shock-level controls (constant, plus period fixed effects panels) exposure weights, instead raw shocks. Point estimates unchanged; standard errors corrected, now match ShiftShareSE’s AKM variance / BHJ ssaggregate workflow exactly (verified test suite). (Point 7.) Shift-route panels now automatically control sum exposure shares interacted period fixed effects (reducing period FE complete shares); controlling overall sum sufficient panels. Auto columns pruned anything user’s controls already span, location-/shock-level equivalence now exact panels. (Point 6.) ssb_rotemberg() gains demean = TRUE: shocks demeaned exposure weights (within periods panels) whenever corresponding constant directions absorbed controls, resolving normalisation non-uniqueness decomposition; weights now invariant adding constants shocks. β̂, per-instrument estimates Fs unaffected. (Point 5.) ssb_pretrend() route-aware: headline se / p / interval exposure-robust shift route conventional (cluster / EHW) share route, three SEs still reported. (Point 2.)

title: ssBartik 0.2.0
text: ssb_estimate(shock_cluster = ) verified genuinely cluster shocks: reaches ShiftShareSE’s sector_cvar matches hand-computed cluster-robust shock-level regression; regression test pins . ssb_shock_iv(cluster = ) now also accepts shocks-table column name. (Point 8.) ssb_ri() documents (prints) randomization inference requires shocks exchangeable within blocks – stronger assumption BHJ -good--random, allows heteroskedastic shocks. (Point 9.) Shock-balance output longer states non-significant test shows shocks “unrelated observables”; reports null balance rejected. (Point 10.) Terminology: high-weight share instruments (sectors), “shocks”, across ssb_weight_summary(), ssb_loo(), ssb_drop_top() output plots. (Point 5.)

title: ssBartik 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-19 First release. end--end toolkit shift-share (Bartik) instrumental variables, spanning identification routes (exogenous shares exogenous shocks) instrument construction estimation, inference, credibility diagnostics. Estimation: ssb_design(), ssbartik(), ssb_estimate(), reporting panel confidence intervals (IID, EHW, exposure-robust AKM / AKM0 default; cluster two-way available request). Rotemberg / GPSS diagnostics: ssb_rotemberg(), ssb_weight_summary(), ssb_loo(), ssb_drop_top(). Shock-level (Borusyak-Hull-Jaravel) tools: ssb_aggregate(), ssb_shock_iv(), ssb_equivalence(), ssb_recenter(). Credibility checks: ssb_first_stage(), ssb_overid(), ssb_share_balance(), ssb_shock_balance(), ssb_pretrend(), ssb_placebo(), ssb_ri(). Output: autoplot() / ssb_plot_*() figures (including ssb_plot_ci() interval comparison), paste-ready tables via format(x, "latex" / "markdown") rendered plot() Rotemberg table.
